title: Heart Murmur in Infant - Ventricular Septal Defect
chief_complaint: 2-month-old with poor weight gain, tachypnea during feeding, and diaphoresis. Loud holosystolic murmur heard at left lower sternal border since birth.

physical_exam.heart: Grade 4/6 holosystolic murmur at LLSB with thrill, loud P2, displaced apex

hidden_data.echo: Large perimembranous VSD 8mm with left-to-right shunt, Qp:Qs 3:1, dilated LA and LV, pulmonary hypertension (RVSP 50mmHg)

correct_diagnosis: Large Ventricular Septal Defect with Pulmonary Hypertension and Heart Failure
key_learning_points: Surgical closure if failure to thrive or pulmonary hypertension

patient_responses.feeding: She gets tired and sweaty during feeds, takes only 2oz
patient_responses.weight: She was 3.5kg at birth, now only 4.2kg at 2 months
patient_responses.breathing: She breathes fast all the time

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Large VSD causing heart failure.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a large VSD. The holosystolic murmur + echo findings confirm it.

echo.comment: Diagnostic - shows VSD size and shunt.

cxr.comment: Shows cardiomegaly and increased pulmonary flow.

feedback.attending_note: Large VSD with CHF: Furosemide 1mg/kg BID, digoxin, increased caloric density feeds. Surgical closure indicated for failure to thrive, pulmonary hypertension, or Qp:Qs >2. Refer to pediatric cardiac surgery. Good catch.
